title: OPID: On-Policy Skill Distillation for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
abstract: arXiv:2606.26790v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Outcome-based reinforcement learning provides a stable optimization backbone for language agents, but its sparse trajectory-level rewards provide little guidance on which intermediate decisions should be reinforced or suppressed. On-policy self-distillation offers dense token-level supervision, yet existing skill-conditioned variants often rely on external skill memories or retrieved privileged context, which are costly to maintain and can be mismatched with the state distribution induced by the current policy in multi-turn interaction. We propose \textbf{OPID} (\textbf{O}n-\textbf{P}olicy Sk\textbf{i}ll \textbf{D}istillation), a framework that extracts skill supervision directly from completed on-policy trajectories. OPID represents trajectory hindsight as hierarchical skills: episode-level skills capture global workflows or failure-avoidance rules, while step-level skills capture local decision knowledge at critical timesteps. A critic

translated_title: OPID：面向智能体强化学习的在线策略技能蒸馏
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26790v1 公告类型：新文 摘要：基于结果的强化学习为语言智能体提供了稳定的优化基础，但其稀疏的轨迹级奖励对于哪些中间决策应该被强化或抑制提供的指导很少。在线策略自蒸馏提供了密集的令牌级监督，然而现有的技能条件变体通常依赖于外部技能记忆或检索到的特权上下文，这些维护成本高昂，并且可能与多轮交互中当前策略所引发的状态分布不匹配。我们提出**OPID**（**在**线策略技**能**蒸馏），一个直接从完成的在线策略轨迹中提取技能监督的框架。OPID将轨迹的后见之明表示为分层技能：情节级技能捕捉全局工作流程或失败规避规则，而步骤级技能捕捉关键时间步的局部决策知识。一个评论家...
tldr: OPID提出一种从在线策略轨迹中直接提取分层技能监督的新方法，通过情节级和步骤级技能分别捕捉全局工作流和局部决策知识，无需依赖外部记忆或特权上下文。
en_tdlr: OPID proposes a novel method to extract hierarchical skill supervision directly from on-policy trajectories, capturing global workflows and local decision knowledge via episode-level and step-level skills without relying on external memory or privileged context.
